qids.subject: Data Structures
qids.prose: Let be a pointer as shown in the figure in a single linked list. What do the following assignment statements achieve? q:= p -> next p -> next:= q -> next q -> next:=(q -> next) -> next (p -> next) -> next:= q

qids.subject: Data Structures
qids.prose: Write a constant time algorithm to insert a node with data just before the node with address of a singly linked list.

qids.subject: Data Structures
qids.prose: In the worst case, the number of comparisons needed to search a single linked list of length for a given element is A. B. C. D.

qids.subject: Data Structures
qids.prose: Consider the function defined below. struct item { int data; struct item * next; }; int f(struct item *p) { return ((p == NULL) || (p->next == NULL)|| ((p->data <= p ->next -> data) && f(p->next))); }
